wiki_catalogs.name: Project Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the xHR Payroll & Finance System project overview. Explain the system's purpose as a replacement for Excel-based payroll management, its target audience including HR professionals and finance teams, and key benefits such as automated calculations, audit trails, and PDF payslip generation. Document the system's core objectives, business value proposition, and how it addresses common payroll challenges. Include both conceptual overviews for beginners and technical highlights for experienced developers. Use terminology consistent with the codebase and provide practical examples demonstrating common use cases.

wiki_catalogs.name: System Introduction
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the xHR Payroll & Finance System introduction. Explain how this system replaces traditional Excel-based payroll management with a modern database-driven application. Document the system's core purpose as a comprehensive payroll solution supporting multiple payroll modes including monthly staff, freelance layer rates, freelance fixed rates, YouTuber/talent payrolls, and custom hybrid models. Detail the key problem it solves - moving away from Excel limitations to automated, auditable, and scalable payroll processing. Include the system's target audience (HR professionals, finance teams, and business owners) and explain how it addresses common payroll challenges like manual calculations, audit trail gaps, and reporting inefficiencies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Overall System Design
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the overall system design of the xHR Payroll & Finance System. Explain the service-oriented architecture pattern, database-first approach, and rule-driven development methodology. Document how the system replaces Excel-based processes with structured database operations. Detail the separation of concerns between different agent roles (Architecture Agent, Database Agent, Payroll Rules Agent, etc.). Include system boundaries, component interactions, and data flow patterns. Provide architectural diagrams showing how payroll modes integrate with the calculation engine and how different modules communicate. Address scalability considerations, performance optimization strategies, and integration points with external systems like Thai Social Security.

wiki_catalogs.name: Record-based Thinking Over Cell-based Excel Logic
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the record-based thinking principle that eliminates Excel spreadsheet anti-patterns. Explain why cell-based positioning logic (B33, X22, jan!X62) must be replaced with proper database records. Document the implementation impact on database design, including proper table relationships and foreign key usage. Provide concrete examples showing the transformation from Excel cell references to structured database records like employee_id, payroll_batch_id, and payroll_item_type. Include anti-pattern prevention guidance with before/after examples demonstrating how this principle prevents data integrity issues and maintains system scalability.

wiki_catalogs.name: Core Payroll Entities
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the core payroll entities section. Document the fundamental domain objects including Employee, EmployeeProfile, EmployeeSalaryProfile, EmployeeBankAccount, PayrollBatch, PayrollItem, AttendanceLog, and WorkLog. Explain each entity's responsibilities, data attributes, and business significance. Detail the entity relationships, primary/foreign key associations, and data flow patterns. Include entity lifecycle management, state transitions, and validation rules. Provide concrete examples of how these entities interact during payroll processing workflows. Document the database schema implications and indexing strategies for optimal performance.

wiki_catalogs.name: Attendance Logs Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the attendance logs management system. Detail the check-in/check-out input functionality including time recording mechanisms, late minute tracking, early leave recording, and overtime calculation rules. Document the attendance log data model with fields for check-in/out timestamps, duration calculations, and status flags. Explain the integration with payroll calculation engine showing how attendance data affects final pay amounts through late deductions, LWOP calculations, and OT processing. Include configuration options for grace periods, minimum hours thresholds, and OT enablement flags. Provide examples of attendance rule configurations and their impact on employee compensation.

wiki_catalogs.name: Employee Profile Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for employee profile management functionality. Thoroughly explain the complete employee registration and profile creation process including personal information capture (name, contact details, ID documents), employment status tracking, and profile validation workflows. Document the employee data model structure with core fields, validation rules, and data integrity constraints. Include configuration options for profile fields, status indicators, and profile lifecycle management. Address common profile management scenarios such as employee onboarding, profile updates, status changes, and data migration processes. Provide practical examples of profile creation workflows and integration with payroll processing systems.

wiki_catalogs.name: Company Finance Tracking
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the company finance tracking system covering revenue management, expense tracking, subscription cost management, and profit/loss reporting. Document the financial data models including company_revenues, company_expenses, subscription_costs, and their relationships. Explain the monthly financial aggregation process, cumulative calculations, quarterly reporting, and tax simulation features. Include detailed coverage of financial summary generation, P&L calculations, and integration with payroll processing. Address the company_monthly_summaries table structure and automated financial reporting workflows.

wiki_catalogs.name: Attendance Rules
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the attendance rules system. Detail the OT calculation rules including minute/hour-based overtime, minimum thresholds, and enable flags. Document late deduction rules covering fixed per-minute penalties, tier-based penalties, and grace periods. Explain LWOP (Leave Without Pay) rules including day-based deductions and proportional salary deductions. Include configuration examples, calculation algorithms, and validation logic. Address how attendance rules integrate with payroll processing and affect net pay calculations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Interface Components
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the main interface components of the xHR Payroll system. Detail the Employee Board interface including card/grid layouts, search functionality, filtering capabilities, and employee selection workflows. Document the Employee Workspace as the primary payroll interface containing headers, month selectors, summary cards, main payroll grid, detail inspector, payslip preview, and audit timeline components. Explain the Rule Manager interface for business configuration and settings management. Include component-specific styling guidelines, responsive design considerations, and integration patterns between components.

wiki_catalogs.name: Monthly Staff Payroll
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the monthly staff payroll mode calculation system. Document the comprehensive calculation formula: total_income = base_salary + overtime_pay + diligence_allowance + performance_bonus + other_income minus total_deduction = cash_advance + late_deduction + lwop_deduction + social_security_employee + other_deduction, resulting in net_pay. Explain the diligence allowance rules (500 THB default when no late minutes and no LWOP days), OT calculation methods (by minute/hour with minimum thresholds), late deduction structures (fixed per minute or tier penalties with grace periods), and LWOP deduction options (day-based or proportional salary). Include practical calculation examples, rule configuration parameters, and integration with attendance tracking system.

wiki_catalogs.name: Audit Trail Implementation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the audit trail implementation covering the mandatory logging fields (who, what, when, old/new values, action, timestamp, optional reason). Document the audit log structure, data capture mechanisms, and storage requirements. Explain the implementation of high-priority audit areas including employee salary profile changes, payroll item modifications, payslip finalize/unfinalize actions, rule changes, module toggle changes, and SSO configuration updates. Include technical specifications for audit log schema, indexing strategies, and performance considerations. Address audit log retention policies, data lifecycle management, and compliance requirements.

wiki_catalogs.name: Coding Standards
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive coding standards documentation for PHP/Laravel development. Document service class organization patterns, controller design principles, validation approaches using FormRequest, and transaction management best practices. Explain naming conventions for classes, methods, and constants. Detail code organization principles including separation of concerns, avoiding God Classes, and proper service layer architecture. Include specific examples of well-structured Laravel components and anti-patterns to avoid. Address code readability, maintainability, and team collaboration standards.

wiki_catalogs.name: Database Conventions and Standards
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for database naming conventions and standards used in the hrX payroll system. Detail table naming patterns (plural snake_case), primary key conventions (id), foreign key naming (<entity>_id), status flags (status/is_active), date field conventions (*_date), duration field naming (*_minutes/*_seconds), monetary field precision (decimal(12,2)), and percentage field standards. Include phpMyAdmin compatibility requirements, migration-friendly design principles, and shared hosting constraints. Document data type selection criteria, indexing strategies, and constraint naming conventions. Provide examples of properly named tables and fields, and explain the rationale behind each convention choice.

wiki_catalogs.name: Rate and Layer Rate Rules
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the rate_rules and layer_rate_rules configuration tables that handle compensation calculations. Document the structure and fields of rate_rules table including rate_type, effective_date, rate_value, and applicable payroll modes. Explain layer_rate_rules for tiered compensation structures with fields like min_amount, max_amount, and rate_percentage. Include examples of rate configuration for different payroll modes including monthly_staff, freelance_layer, and custom_hybrid. Provide implementation patterns for rate lookup algorithms and validation rules for rate consistency across different employee types.

wiki_catalogs.name: Service Layer Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the service layer architecture in the xHR Payroll & Finance System. Document the core service classes including PayrollCalculationService, EmployeeService, AttendanceService, WorkLogService, BonusRuleService, SocialSecurityService, PayslipService, CompanyFinanceService, and AuditLogService. Explain the service-oriented architecture pattern, dependency injection, and how services interact with models and repositories. Detail the service boundaries, responsibilities, and communication patterns between services. Include examples of service method signatures, parameter validation, and error handling strategies. Document the transaction management approach and how services ensure data consistency across payroll calculations and financial operations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Employee Entity
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Employee entity. Detail the employee profile structure including personal information fields, employment status tracking, and payroll mode assignment. Document the entity's role as the central hub for all payroll-related activities and its relationships with related entities like EmployeeProfile, EmployeeSalaryProfile, and EmployeeBankAccount. Explain the employee lifecycle from onboarding to offboarding, status management, and department/position assignments. Include validation rules, data integrity constraints, and business logic governing employee data access and modifications. Provide examples of common operations like employee creation, status updates, and payroll mode changes.

wiki_catalogs.name: Bonus Rules
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the BonusRule entity and ThresholdRule configuration system. Explain how performance-based bonuses are configured through threshold-based rules, including rule definition, evaluation logic, and calculation algorithms. Document the relationship between performance metrics and bonus payouts, including minimum thresholds, tiered bonus structures, and maximum bonus limits. Include examples of common bonus scenarios such as sales targets, productivity thresholds, and performance ratings. Detail the rule validation process, dependency checking between rules, and how bonus calculations integrate with payroll processing. Address configuration management, rule precedence, and audit trail implications for bonus modifications.

wiki_catalogs.name: Overtime Calculation Rules
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the overtime calculation rules system. Document the OT calculation modes including minute-based and hour-based overtime computation. Explain the minimum threshold configuration, enable/disable flags, and calculation algorithms. Detail how overtime is calculated from attendance logs, including how late minutes are converted to overtime hours. Include configuration examples showing different OT rate structures, grace period handling, and integration with payroll processing. Address validation logic, edge cases, and how OT affects net pay calculations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Performance Bonus Rules
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the performance bonus rules system. Thoroughly explain threshold-based bonus calculations including minimum performance requirements, tier structures with progressive bonus rates, and eligibility criteria for different employee categories. Document the bonus rule configuration process, including how to define performance thresholds, bonus percentages, and calculation algorithms. Include concrete examples from the actual codebase showing bonus calculation formulas, rule validation logic, and integration with payroll processing. Address how performance data feeds into bonus calculations, including attendance-based performance metrics and work quality indicators. Document the relationship between performance bonuses and other payroll components like base salary and overtime pay.

wiki_catalogs.name: Payslip and Payroll Item Entities
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Payslip and PayslipItems entities that form the core of payroll output generation. Detail the payslip structure including company header, employee details, month, payment date, account information, income and deduction sections, and totals. Document the payslip_items table structure with income types, deduction types, and their relationships to payroll_items. Explain the payslip finalization workflow, snapshot mechanism, and PDF generation process. Include examples of payslip data flow from payroll calculation to final document output, covering the critical rule that reductions must appear in deductions, not as base salary reductions. Document the relationship between payslip snapshots and audit trail preservation.

wiki_catalogs.name: Employee Board Interface
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Employee Board interface component. Detail the card/grid layout design, search functionality implementation, filtering capabilities by role/status/mode, and employee selection workflows. Document the user flow from login to dashboard to employee board to individual employee workspace. Explain the visual presentation of employee information, status indicators, and quick action buttons. Include responsive design considerations, accessibility features, and integration patterns with the backend employee management system. Provide examples of common user interactions and navigation patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Basic Calculation Formula
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the fundamental monthly staff payroll calculation formula. Document the core equation: total_income = base_salary + overtime_pay + diligence_allowance + performance_bonus + other_income and total_deduction = cash_advance + late_deduction + lwop_deduction + social_security_employee + other_deduction, resulting in net_pay = total_income - total_deduction. Explain each component's role in the calculation chain, provide step-by-step calculation examples, and demonstrate how manual overrides and rule-generated values interact within this framework.

wiki_catalogs.name: System Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for the xHR Payroll & Finance System. Describe the high-level design principles including service-oriented architecture, Laravel framework utilization, and database-first approach. Document component interactions, data flows between payroll calculation engine, employee management, and financial reporting modules. Explain technical decisions around PHP-first development, MySQL database design, and rule-driven configuration system. Include infrastructure requirements, scalability considerations, and integration patterns with external systems like Thai Social Security. Provide system context diagrams showing how different payroll modes interact with the calculation engine.

wiki_catalogs.name: Key Features
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the xHR Payroll & Finance System key features. Document the multi-payroll mode support including monthly staff payroll, freelance layer rate payroll, freelance fixed rate payroll, YouTuber/talent salary payroll, YouTuber/talent settlement payroll, and custom hybrid payroll modes. Explain the dynamic data entry system with Excel-like interface capabilities, rule-driven business logic configuration, comprehensive audit trail system, PDF payslip generation functionality, annual and company financial summaries, and attendance and work log tracking. Include practical examples of how each feature benefits users and demonstrates the system's comprehensive approach to payroll management.

wiki_catalogs.name: Technology Stack and Constraints
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the technology stack and constraints of the xHR Payroll & Finance System. Document the required PHP 8.2+ runtime, Laravel framework preferences, MySQL 8+ database requirements, and phpMyAdmin compatibility standards. Explain the frontend technology choices including Blade templating, vanilla JS, Alpine.js, and PDF generation libraries (DomPDF/Snappy). Detail the architectural constraints that prevent spreadsheet-as-database patterns, magic numbers in views, and business logic in controllers. Include compatibility requirements for shared hosting environments, migration system constraints, and third-party library integration points. Address version compatibility matrices and upgrade paths for each technology component.

wiki_catalogs.name: Single Source of Truth Principle
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the single source of truth principle that establishes authoritative data sources. Document the authoritative data locations for all major entities including employees, salary profiles, rate configurations, payroll items, payslips, and company finances. Explain the database table mapping and how each business entity maintains its master record. Include implementation examples showing proper data flow from authoritative sources to derived calculations and reports. Address the anti-patterns of duplicate data sources and explain how this principle ensures data consistency across the entire system.

wiki_catalogs.name: Business Rule Entities
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for business rule entities including BonusRule, ThresholdRule, SocialSecurityConfig, RateRules, LayerRateRules, and ModuleToggle. Explain how these entities enable the rule-driven architecture by storing configurable business logic separately from code. Detail the rule evaluation mechanisms, dependency relationships between different rule types, and how rules cascade through the payroll calculation process. Include examples of rule configuration scenarios, validation requirements, and audit trail implications. Document the relationship between rule entities and payroll processing outcomes.

wiki_catalogs.name: Work Log System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the work log system used primarily for freelancers and hybrid payroll modes. Document the work log data model including date fields, work type categorization, quantity/time measurements, layer rate calculations, and amount computations. Explain the work log types table structure and how different work categories are configured. Detail the layer rate calculation algorithm showing how minutes, quantities, and rates combine to produce amounts. Include examples of work log entry workflows, validation rules, and integration with the payroll calculation engine. Document the relationship between work logs and final compensation for freelance staff.

wiki_catalogs.name: Employment Status Tracking
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for employment status tracking functionality. Thoroughly explain the employment lifecycle management including active, inactive, probationary, and terminated status tracking. Document status transition workflows, approval processes, and audit trail requirements for status changes. Include configuration options for status types, transition rules, and notification triggers. Address common status management scenarios such as employee termination procedures, rehiring processes, and status validation requirements. Provide practical examples of status change workflows and integration with payroll calculation systems.

wiki_catalogs.name: Payslip Generation System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the payslip generation system covering payslip structure components, PDF creation workflow, snapshot mechanism, and finalization process. Document the payslip data model including payslips and payslip_items tables, their relationships, and the snapshot rule implementation. Explain the payslip rendering process, template system, and PDF generation using DomPDF/Snappy. Cover the critical rule that prevents reducing payments by modifying base salary, the source flag system (auto, manual, override, master), and audit trail requirements for payslip modifications. Include payslip preview functionality and finalization workflow.

wiki_catalogs.name: Compensation Rules
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the compensation rules system. Thoroughly explain performance bonus rules including threshold-based bonus calculations, tier structures, and eligibility criteria. Document layer rate calculation rules for freelance work including minute-based calculations, rate tiers, and amount determinations. Cover fixed-rate compensation rules for different work types. Include rule configuration examples, calculation formulas, and integration with payroll modes. Address how compensation rules interact with attendance and performance data.

wiki_catalogs.name: Dynamic Grid Behavior
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the dynamic grid system that mimics Excel functionality while maintaining proper data governance. Document the main payroll grid capabilities including inline editing, row manipulation (add/remove/duplicate), dropdown type/category inputs, automatic amount calculation, manual override functionality, and real-time recalculation. Explain the grid's state management system showing field states (locked, auto, manual, override, from_master, rule_applied, draft, finalized). Include the detail inspector functionality that appears when clicking rows, showing source information, formula/rule sources, monthly-only vs master indicators, note/reason capabilities, and audit history display.

wiki_catalogs.name: Freelance Layer Rate Payroll
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the freelance layer rate payroll calculation system. Document the standard formula: duration_minutes = minute + (second / 60) and amount = duration_minutes * rate_per_minute. Explain the layer rate calculation methodology, work log integration for tracking work types, quantities, and rates, and how the system handles time-based compensation for freelancers. Include configuration examples for different work categories, rate structures, and calculation precision requirements. Address edge cases like partial minutes, rounding rules, and integration with the work log module.

wiki_catalogs.name: Compliance Requirements
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for compliance requirements covering regulatory compliance obligations, legal frameworks, and industry standards. Document the relationship between audit logs and regulatory reporting, data protection requirements, and retention policies. Explain compliance validation processes, audit readiness procedures, and forensic audit preparation capabilities. Include coverage of Thai labor law compliance, social security regulations, tax reporting requirements, and financial statement accuracy. Address data governance policies, privacy considerations, and access control mechanisms required for compliance.

wiki_catalogs.name: Testing Strategy
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed testing strategy documentation covering unit testing, integration testing, and business logic validation approaches. Document minimum test requirements for payroll mode calculations, SSO calculations, layer rate computations, payslip snapshot functionality, and audit logging. Explain testing methodologies for different components including services, controllers, and database operations. Include test case organization, mocking strategies, and continuous integration practices. Address test coverage requirements and quality assurance procedures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Core Table Structure and Relationships
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the core database table structure and entity relationships in the hrX payroll system. Document all suggested tables including users, roles, permissions, employees, employee_profiles, employee_salary_profiles, employee_bank_accounts, departments, positions, payroll_batches, payroll_items, payroll_item_types, attendance_logs, work_logs, work_log_types, rate_rules, layer_rate_rules, bonus_rules, threshold_rules, social_security_configs, expense_claims, company_revenues, company_expenses, subscription_costs, payslips, payslip_items, module_toggles, and audit_logs. Explain table relationships, foreign key constraints, and referential integrity. Include entity relationship diagrams showing connections between core payroll entities. Document primary key relationships, cascading behaviors, and join patterns essential for payroll processing.

wiki_catalogs.name: Bonus and Threshold Rules
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for bonus_rules and threshold_rules configuration tables. Detail the structure of bonus_rules including performance_metric, target_value, bonus_amount, and bonus_type fields. Document threshold_rules for performance-based bonuses with min_threshold, max_threshold, and bonus_percentage configurations. Include examples of rule configuration for different performance scenarios including tiered bonuses, percentage-based bonuses, and fixed amount bonuses. Explain the integration with performance tracking systems and how rules are applied during payroll calculation.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Flow and Processing Workflows
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for data flow patterns and processing workflows in the xHR system. Document the complete data journey from user input through validation, business rule processing, calculation engine execution, and final payslip generation. Explain the flow of data between Employee Board, Employee Workspace, Rule Manager, and Payslip modules. Detail the payroll calculation workflow including monthly staff processing, freelance calculations, and youtuber/talent settlements. Include sequence diagrams showing typical user interactions, data validation steps, and audit trail propagation. Address error handling, transaction boundaries, and consistency guarantees across the entire data processing pipeline.

wiki_catalogs.name: PayrollBatch Entity
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the PayrollBatch entity. Explain its role as a container for payroll processing cycles, including batch creation, status management, and processing workflows. Document the batch lifecycle from initiation through completion, including validation requirements, processing dependencies, and error handling mechanisms. Detail the relationship between payroll batches and individual payroll items, including how batches coordinate multi-employee processing. Cover batch-level configuration options, processing constraints, and audit trail requirements. Include examples of batch creation, processing workflows, and status transitions throughout the payroll cycle.

wiki_catalogs.name: Social Security Configuration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the SocialSecurityConfig entity covering Thailand social security compliance requirements. Detail the configuration parameters including employee contribution rates, employer contribution rates, salary ceiling limits, and maximum monthly contributions. Explain the effective date management system for rule updates, rate calculation algorithms, and integration with payroll processing. Document the relationship between salary profiles and social security calculations, including proration for partial months and special circumstances. Include examples of configuration scenarios for different employment statuses and salary structures. Address compliance requirements, regulatory changes handling, and audit trail for social security modifications.

wiki_catalogs.name: Late Deduction Rules
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the late deduction rules system. Document the fixed per-minute penalty structure, tier-based penalty configurations, and grace period settings. Explain how late minutes are tracked from attendance logs and converted to monetary deductions. Detail the calculation algorithms for different penalty structures, including how tier rates apply to different late minute ranges. Include configuration examples showing penalty rate variations, grace period handling, and integration with payroll deductions. Address validation logic, maximum deduction limits, and impact on net pay calculations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Layer Rate Calculation Rules
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the layer rate calculation rules used in freelance compensation. Thoroughly explain minute-based calculation methodology including rate tiers, progressive pricing structures, and amount determination algorithms. Document the layer rate configuration process, including how to define different rate levels, tier breakpoints, and calculation formulas. Include concrete examples from the actual codebase showing layer rate calculations for various work types, including work log integration and duration-based billing. Address how layer rates interact with work logs, including minute rounding rules, partial hour calculations, and tier progression logic. Document the relationship between layer rates and other freelance compensation components, including fixed-rate alternatives and hybrid calculation methods.

wiki_catalogs.name: Expense Claim and Company Financial Entities
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for ExpenseClaim, CompanyRevenue, CompanyExpense, and SubscriptionCost entities that support comprehensive financial tracking. Explain how expense claims integrate with payroll processing for freelancer and settlement calculations. Document the company financial tracking system including revenue recognition, expense categorization, and subscription cost management. Detail the relationship between expense claims and payslip generation, particularly for YouTuber/Talent settlement payroll mode. Include examples of financial workflow integration showing how expenses flow from claim approval through company expense tracking to profit/loss calculation. Document the data models for subscription costs and their impact on monthly financial summaries.

wiki_catalogs.name: Employee Workspace Interface
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the Employee Workspace interface, the primary payroll processing interface. Document all major components including the header section, month selector functionality, summary cards display, main payroll grid with inline editing capabilities, detail inspector panel, payslip preview area, and audit timeline component. Explain the dynamic UI behaviors, state management for different field types (locked, auto, manual, override), real-time calculation feedback, and source tracking indicators. Include workflow patterns for payroll entry, editing, and validation processes.

wiki_catalogs.name: Diligence Allowance System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the diligence allowance calculation system in monthly staff payroll. Document the default allowance of 500 THB when employee has zero late minutes and zero LWOP days. Explain the allowance calculation logic, configuration parameters, eligibility criteria, and integration with attendance tracking. Include practical examples showing different scenarios (with late minutes, with LWOP days, normal cases) and demonstrate how the allowance interacts with other payroll components.

wiki_catalogs.name: Core Design Principles
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the core design principles that guide the xHR system development. Thoroughly explain the six fundamental principles: record-based thinking over cell-based Excel logic, single source of truth for all data, rule-driven instead of hardcoded business logic, dynamic but controlled editing interface, and maintainability-first approach. Document implementation details for each principle, including database design implications, UI behavior patterns, and development workflow impacts. Include concrete examples from the actual system showing how these principles prevent common Excel-related anti-patterns and ensure system integrity.

wiki_catalogs.name: Target Audience
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed content for identifying and describing the target audience for the xHR Payroll & Finance System. Document the primary users including HR professionals managing employee compensation, finance teams handling payroll processing and reporting, business owners needing financial insights, and administrators configuring system rules and permissions. Explain the secondary users such as employees accessing their payslips, managers reviewing team compensation, and compliance officers ensuring regulatory adherence. Include user skill levels, technical comfort zones, and specific pain points each audience segment faces with traditional Excel-based systems. Provide use case scenarios that demonstrate how different roles benefit from the system's capabilities.

wiki_catalogs.name: Component Interactions and Data Flow
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for component interactions and data flow patterns in the xHR Payroll & Finance System. Document the interaction between payroll calculation engine, employee management services, attendance tracking modules, and financial reporting systems. Explain data flow from user input through validation, business rule processing, calculation engine execution, and final payslip generation. Detail the service layer architecture with PayrollCalculationService, EmployeeService, AttendanceService, and other core services. Include sequence diagrams showing typical payroll processing workflows, audit trail propagation, and state management across components. Address error handling, transaction boundaries, and consistency guarantees between interacting components.

wiki_catalogs.name: Rule-driven Instead of Hardcoded Business Logic
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the rule-driven development principle that replaces hardcoded business logic with configurable rules. Document the rule configuration tables including rate_rules, layer_rate_rules, bonus_rules, threshold_rules, and social_security_configs. Explain how business logic is extracted from views and controllers into maintainable rule configurations. Provide implementation examples showing the transformation from hardcoded formulas to database-configurable rules. Include guidance on rule validation, dependency management, and the benefits of this approach for system maintainability and flexibility.

wiki_catalogs.name: Financial and Reporting Entities
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Document the financial management entities including Payslip, PayslipItems, ExpenseClaim, CompanyRevenue, CompanyExpense, SubscriptionCost, and CompanyMonthlySummaries. Explain how these entities support the financial reporting and compliance requirements. Detail the relationship between payroll results and financial statements, including revenue recognition, expense tracking, and profit/loss calculation. Include documentation of financial data aggregation, reporting requirements, and audit trail preservation for financial records. Provide examples of financial workflow integration with payroll processing.

wiki_catalogs.name: Department and Position Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for department and position management functionality. Thoroughly explain organizational structure management including department creation, position assignment, and hierarchical relationships. Document department and position data models, validation rules, and reporting structure configurations. Include configuration options for organizational hierarchies, position classifications, and department cost center assignments. Address common organizational management scenarios such as department transfers, position changes, and reporting relationships. Provide practical examples of organizational structure setup and integration with payroll and reporting systems.

wiki_catalogs.name: Social Security Configuration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for Thailand-specific social security configuration. Detail the configurable rates including employee contribution rates, employer contribution rates, salary ceilings, and maximum monthly contributions. Document effective date management for rule changes and compliance requirements. Explain the integration with payroll calculations and how social security amounts are calculated and deducted. Include configuration examples, compliance guidelines, and audit requirements for social security settings.

wiki_catalogs.name: State Management and Visual Indicators
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the state management system and visual indicators throughout the user interface. Document all field and row states including locked, auto, manual, override, from_master, rule_applied, draft, and finalized states. Explain how state indicators appear as badges, color coding, and visual cues in the grid interface. Detail the source flag system (auto, manual, override, master) and how it's displayed to users. Include guidelines for state transitions, user feedback mechanisms, and the audit trail integration that tracks state changes. Address accessibility considerations for state visualization and screen reader compatibility.

wiki_catalogs.name: Freelance Fixed Rate Payroll
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the freelance fixed rate payroll calculation system. Document the straightforward formula: amount = quantity * fixed_rate. Explain how this mode handles predetermined compensation structures for freelancers, work log requirements, and integration with the work log module. Include examples of different work types that use fixed rates, quantity tracking mechanisms, and how the system validates fixed rate configurations against business rules.

wiki_catalogs.name: Change Management Process
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the change management process including the five-question evaluation framework (master vs monthly, affected payroll modes, impact on payslip/report/finance, migration requirements, additional audit/test coverage). Document approval workflows, rollback capabilities, and change validation procedures. Explain the relationship between change management and audit requirements, including pre-change impact assessment and post-change verification. Include guidelines for handling emergency changes, version control integration, and deployment strategies that maintain system integrity while enabling necessary modifications.

wiki_catalogs.name: Development Workflow
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed development workflow documentation covering project structure organization, folder structure guidance, and component architecture. Document recommended Laravel structure with Models, Services, Actions, Enums, Controllers, Requests, and Policies. Explain service class organization including EmployeeService, PayrollCalculationService, AttendanceService, WorkLogService, BonusRuleService, SocialSecurityService, PayslipService, CompanyFinanceService, AuditLogService, and ModuleToggleService. Include branching strategies, code review processes, and deployment procedures. Address team collaboration and project management practices.

wiki_catalogs.name: phpMyAdmin Compatibility and Shared Hosting
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for phpMyAdmin compatibility requirements and shared hosting considerations for the hrX payroll system database. Explain design principles that ensure easy table viewing, readable field names, and basic query debugging capabilities. Document limitations and constraints imposed by shared hosting environments, including MySQL version compatibility (8+), storage engine requirements, and feature restrictions. Detail migration compatibility, schema evolution patterns, and database administration best practices. Include troubleshooting guides for common phpMyAdmin issues, query optimization for shared hosting, and backup/restore procedures. Address performance considerations, indexing strategies, and database maintenance tasks suitable for shared hosting environments.

wiki_catalogs.name: Attendance and Deduction Rules
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Document the attendance-related rules configuration including OT calculation rules, late deduction rules, and LWOP (Leave Without Pay) rules. Detail the structure of attendance rules with fields for overtime_calculation_method, late_tolerance_minutes, deduction_per_minute, and LWOP_calculation_basis. Include examples of rule configuration for different attendance scenarios including minute-based OT, tiered penalties, and proportional salary deductions. Explain the integration with attendance_log system and how rules are validated against actual attendance data.

wiki_catalogs.name: Component State Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed content for component state management and coordination in the xHR Payroll & Finance System. Document the various UI states including locked, auto, manual, override, from_master, rule_applied, draft, and finalized states. Explain how state transitions occur during payroll processing, payslip editing, and rule configuration. Detail the state management patterns used in the dynamic grid interface, employee workspace, and audit timeline components. Include examples of state persistence, validation triggers, and UI feedback mechanisms. Address how state information flows between components and maintains consistency across the user interface.

wiki_catalogs.name: PayrollItem Entity
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the PayrollItem entity. Detail the structure of individual payroll components including income items, deductions, and allowances. Document the item classification system, source tracking (auto/manual/override), and calculation methodology. Explain the relationship between payroll items and their corresponding payroll batch, employee, and rule configurations. Cover item state management, approval workflows, and audit trail requirements. Include validation rules for amount calculations, source verification, and business logic enforcement. Provide examples of different item types, calculation scenarios, and state transitions throughout the payroll processing workflow.

wiki_catalogs.name: Rate Rules and Layer Rate Rules
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for RateRules and LayerRateRules entities used in freelance and performance-based compensation calculations. Explain the rate configuration system including fixed rates, layered rate structures, and tier-based pricing models. Document the calculation algorithms for layer rate scenarios, including how duration-based work translates to monetary amounts through rate tiers. Detail the relationship between work logs, rate configurations, and final compensation calculations. Include examples of common layer rate scenarios such as video editing time rates, content creation billing, and performance-based pricing. Address rate validation, tier precedence, and integration with work log processing and payroll calculation workflows.

wiki_catalogs.name: Leave Without Pay (LWOP) Rules
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the Leave Without Pay (LWOP) deduction rules system. Document the day-based deduction structure and proportional salary deduction methods. Explain how LWOP days are tracked from attendance logs and converted to monetary deductions. Detail the calculation algorithms for different deduction approaches, including prorated daily salary calculations and full-day deductions. Include configuration examples showing LWOP rate structures, maximum deduction limits, and integration with payroll processing. Address validation logic, minimum wage considerations, and impact on net pay calculations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Company Monthly Financial Summary
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the CompanyMonthlySummaries entity and related financial reporting functionality. Explain how the system aggregates payroll results into company financial statements including revenue, expenses, profit/loss calculations, and cumulative tracking. Document the quarterly reporting capabilities, tax simulation features, and integration with external accounting systems. Detail the data aggregation processes that convert individual payroll results into consolidated financial reports. Include examples of financial reporting workflows showing how monthly payroll processing feeds into annual financial summaries. Document the audit trail requirements for financial reporting and compliance with Thai accounting standards.

wiki_catalogs.name: Rule Manager Interface
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the Rule Manager interface used for business configuration and settings management. Document the rule categories including attendance rules, OT rules, bonus rules, threshold rules, layer rate rules, SSO rules, tax rules, and module toggles. Explain the configuration forms, validation mechanisms, rule dependency management, and real-time impact assessment. Include user workflows for adding, editing, and testing rule configurations, along with the audit trail for rule changes and their implications on payroll calculations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Overtime Calculation Rules
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the monthly staff overtime calculation system. Document the three supported OT calculation methods: OT by minute, OT by hour, and minimum threshold requirements. Explain the calculation algorithms, rate structures, enable flags, and integration with attendance logs. Include practical examples showing different OT scenarios, threshold calculations, and how OT affects net pay. Address grace periods, approval workflows, and audit trail requirements for OT adjustments.

wiki_catalogs.name: Domain Model and Entities
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive data model documentation for the xHR payroll system domain entities. Detail the core business entities including Employee, EmployeeProfile, EmployeeSalaryProfile, PayrollBatch, PayrollItem, AttendanceLog, WorkLog, and their relationships. Document entity responsibilities, data flow patterns, and processing logic for each domain object. Include database schema considerations, field definitions, and business rule enforcement. Explain how different payroll modes affect entity relationships and data storage patterns. Provide entity relationship diagrams and sample data structures showing typical payroll processing scenarios.

wiki_catalogs.name: Business Benefits
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive content for the business benefits of implementing the xHR Payroll & Finance System. Document the quantifiable advantages including reduced manual calculation errors, automated compliance with Thai social security regulations, improved audit trail capabilities, streamlined payslip generation and distribution, enhanced reporting and analytics capabilities, and increased operational efficiency. Explain cost savings through reduced manual labor hours, decreased risk of compliance violations, improved employee satisfaction through accurate and timely payments, and better decision-making support through comprehensive financial reporting. Include implementation timeline expectations, ROI projections, and risk mitigation benefits. Address how the system supports business growth and scalability.

wiki_catalogs.name: Integration Patterns and External Systems
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for integration patterns and external system connectivity in the xHR Payroll & Finance System. Document integration points with Thai Social Security systems, banking direct deposit interfaces, tax authority reporting systems, and HRIS system synchronization. Explain API design patterns for external integrations, data transformation requirements, and error handling strategies. Detail webhook implementations, batch processing capabilities, and real-time vs asynchronous integration approaches. Include security considerations for external communications, authentication mechanisms, and data privacy compliance. Address integration testing strategies, mock service implementations, and fallback mechanisms for external system failures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Dynamic but Controlled Editing Interface
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive content for the dynamic but controlled editing principle that combines Excel-like user experience with proper system controls. Document the supported editing behaviors including inline editing, row addition/removal, duplication, and instant recalculation. Explain the mandatory controls including source flags (auto, manual, override, master), audit logging, permission control, and validation requirements. Provide implementation examples showing how to create spreadsheet-like interfaces while maintaining data integrity and traceability. Include user experience guidelines and technical implementation patterns for controlled dynamic editing.

wiki_catalogs.name: Entity Relationship Mapping
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed entity relationship documentation showing how all domain objects connect and interact within the xHR system. Document the complete entity relationship diagram with cardinalities, foreign key constraints, and referential integrity rules. Explain the data flow patterns between entities during different payroll modes and processing scenarios. Detail the inheritance hierarchies, polymorphic associations, and composite entities. Include relationship validation rules, cascade behaviors, and performance optimization strategies. Provide examples of common query patterns and join strategies used throughout the system.

wiki_catalogs.name: Bank Account Configuration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for bank account configuration functionality. Thoroughly explain employee bank account setup including account number, bank code, branch information, and account type configurations. Document bank account validation processes, account verification workflows, and direct deposit integration requirements. Include configuration options for bank account types, account status tracking, and account change procedures. Address common banking scenarios such as account updates, bank transfers, and account verification processes. Provide practical examples of bank account setup workflows and integration with payroll disbursement systems.

wiki_catalogs.name: User Workflows and Interaction Patterns
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Document the complete user interaction patterns and workflow sequences within the xHR Payroll system. Detail the Employee Board flow from login through dashboard to employee selection. Explain the payroll entry flow from Employee Workspace through grid editing, recalculation, payslip preview, saving, and finalization. Document inline editing behaviors, real-time calculation feedback mechanisms, and immediate payslip preview functionality. Include user journey mapping, task completion patterns, error handling during interactions, and recovery mechanisms. Address mobile responsiveness, touch interaction patterns, keyboard navigation, and accessibility features for different user needs.

wiki_catalogs.name: YouTuber/Talent Salary Payroll
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the YouTuber/Talent salary payroll mode. Explain how this mode operates similarly to monthly staff payroll but with specialized configurations for content creators and talent management. Document the payroll calculation similarities, module-specific settings, and how the system handles the unique aspects of talent compensation including performance-based components, royalty structures, and content-specific bonuses. Include configuration examples and integration requirements with the broader payroll system.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Governance and Retention
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for data governance and retention policies covering data lifecycle management, archival strategies, and access control mechanisms. Document retention schedules for different types of payroll data, audit logs, and configuration information. Explain data classification schemes, sensitivity levels, and appropriate handling procedures. Include coverage of data minimization principles, secure deletion processes, and compliance with data protection regulations. Address backup strategies, disaster recovery procedures, and data integrity verification mechanisms.

wiki_catalogs.name: Quality Assurance
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Document quality assurance procedures including code review processes, anti-pattern detection, and technical debt management. Explain the five-question change management framework for evaluating system modifications. Detail quality checkpoints, peer review standards, and continuous improvement practices. Include guidelines for maintaining code quality, preventing technical debt, and ensuring system maintainability. Address debugging strategies, performance optimization, and system reliability practices.

wiki_catalogs.name: Social Security Configuration Rules
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for social_security_configs table that handles Thailand social security compliance rules. Document the configuration structure including employee_contribution_rate, employer_contribution_rate, salary_ceiling, max_monthly_contribution, and effective_date fields. Include examples of rule configuration for different social security periods and how the system handles rate changes over time. Explain the integration with payroll calculation engine and compliance requirements for accurate SSO deduction calculations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Audit Trail and Integration Patterns
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive content for audit trail implementation and integration patterns throughout the xHR system. Document how audit logging integrates with all major components including employee management, payroll processing, rule configuration, and payslip finalization. Explain the audit log structure with who, what entity, what field, old value, new value, action, timestamp, and optional reason fields. Detail the integration points where audit events are triggered, the audit log service coordination, and how audit trails support compliance requirements. Include examples of audit event generation, historical record maintenance, and rollback capability implementation across different payroll modes and business operations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Attendance and Work Log Entities
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for AttendanceLog and WorkLog entities. Explain the time-tracking functionality including check-in/check-out systems, duration calculations, and work type classifications. Document the relationship between attendance records and payroll calculations, including OT computation, late deductions, and LWOP processing. Detail work log management for freelancers and contractors, including layer rate calculations and hourly billing systems. Cover validation rules for time entries, work type categorization, and integration with payroll processing workflows. Include examples of attendance recording, work log entry, OT calculation, and their impact on final payroll computations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Module Toggle Configuration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the ModuleToggle entity that controls feature availability and system functionality. Explain how module toggles enable selective activation of payroll modes, business features, and system capabilities. Document the configuration management system including toggle states, dependency relationships between modules, and impact assessment for module changes. Detail the integration with payroll processing, UI rendering, and business logic execution. Include examples of common module toggle scenarios such as enabling/disabling specific payroll modes, activating/deactivating business rules, and controlling feature visibility. Address the audit trail for module changes, rollback procedures, and system-wide impact analysis.

wiki_catalogs.name: Payslip Preview Interface
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Document the Payslip Preview interface component responsible for displaying and managing payslip generation and review processes. Detail the payslip layout structure, real-time preview functionality, editing capabilities before finalization, PDF export options, and the snapshot mechanism for finalized payslips. Explain the relationship between preview data and the underlying payslip database records, validation processes, and the workflow from preview to finalization. Include user guidance for reviewing payslip details and making corrections.

wiki_catalogs.name: Late Deduction System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the monthly staff late deduction calculation system. Document the two supported deduction methods: fixed per minute deductions and tier penalty structures. Explain grace period configurations, deduction thresholds, and progressive penalty calculations. Include practical examples showing different late minute scenarios, penalty tier applications, and how late deductions impact net pay. Address integration with attendance tracking and policy compliance requirements.

wiki_catalogs.name: Payroll Processing System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the payroll processing system covering all six supported payroll modes. Document the multi-payroll mode architecture including monthly staff, freelance layer rate, freelance fixed rate, YouTuber/talent salary, YouTuber/talent settlement, and custom hybrid modes. Explain calculation algorithms, business rule implementations, and data processing workflows for each mode. Include detailed examples of calculation formulas, rule configurations, and edge case handling. Document the payroll calculation engine's modular design and how different modes share common infrastructure while supporting unique business logic.

wiki_catalogs.name: Maintainability First Approach
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed content for the maintainability-first approach that prioritizes future extensibility and system improvement. Document the specific maintainability requirements including adding new payroll modes, introducing new rules, modifying social security ceilings, customizing payslip formats, and adding new reports. Explain the architectural patterns and design decisions that support easy system enhancement. Include implementation examples showing modular design patterns, plugin-style configurations, and extensible service architectures. Address the long-term benefits of this approach for system evolution and technical debt management.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Flow and Processing Patterns
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for data flow patterns and processing logic across the domain model. Explain how data moves through the system from initial entry through final payslip generation. Document the state management patterns, data transformation processes, and validation pipelines. Detail the master vs transaction vs result data patterns, source flag management, and data lineage tracking. Include examples of complex processing scenarios like hybrid payroll modes, rule cascading effects, and audit trail generation. Explain the performance implications of different data flow architectures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Payroll Mode Assignment
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for payroll mode assignment functionality. Thoroughly explain the payroll mode selection process including monthly staff, freelance layer rate, freelance fixed rate, youtuber/talent payroll modes, and custom hybrid configurations. Document payroll mode assignment workflows, eligibility requirements, and mode-specific configuration processes. Include configuration options for payroll mode types, mode activation/deactivation, and mode-specific parameter settings. Address common payroll mode scenarios such as employee classification, mode transitions, and mode validation requirements. Provide practical examples of payroll mode assignment workflows and integration with calculation engines.

wiki_catalogs.name: YouTuber/Talent Settlement Payroll
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the YouTuber/Talent settlement payroll calculation system. Document the settlement formula: net = total_income - total_expense, explaining how this mode handles profit-and-loss settlements for content creators. Detail the income tracking mechanisms, expense categorization, and how the system calculates net settlement amounts. Include examples of typical income and expense items for YouTuber/Talent, settlement calculation workflows, and integration with the company finance tracking system.

wiki_catalogs.name: Module Toggle and System Configuration Rules
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Document the module_toggles table and system-wide configuration rules that control feature availability and system behavior. Detail the structure including module_name, is_enabled, default_value, and dependency_fields. Include examples of rule configuration for enabling/disabling payroll modes, controlling audit logging, and managing system-wide settings. Explain the impact of module toggles on payroll processing and how they affect rule application across different payroll modes.

wiki_catalogs.name: Financial and Reporting Entities
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for financial entities including Payslip, CompanyRevenue, CompanyExpense, and related financial reporting components. Detail the payslip structure, itemization requirements, and PDF generation workflow. Document company financial tracking including revenue streams, expense categories, and subscription cost management. Explain the relationship between payroll items and financial reporting, including tax implications and regulatory compliance. Cover financial data aggregation, reporting requirements, and audit trail maintenance for financial transactions. Include examples of payslip generation, financial statement preparation, and compliance reporting workflows.

wiki_catalogs.name: Leave Without Pay Deduction
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the Leave Without Pay (LWOP) deduction system in monthly staff payroll. Document the two supported deduction methods: day-based deductions and proportional salary deductions. Explain the calculation algorithms, LWOP day tracking, and integration with attendance logs. Include practical examples showing different LWOP scenarios, deduction calculations, and how LWOP affects monthly net pay. Address policy compliance and documentation requirements.

wiki_catalogs.name: Employee Management System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the employee management system functionality. Thoroughly explain employee profile management including personal information, employment status tracking, department and position assignments, bank account configurations, and social security eligibility setup. Document the employee onboarding workflow, profile update processes, and status change management. Include configuration options for payroll mode assignment, salary structure setup, and role-based access control. Address common employee management scenarios, data validation requirements, and integration with payroll calculation processes.

wiki_catalogs.name: Social Security Eligibility Setup
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for social security eligibility setup functionality. Thoroughly explain Thailand social security configuration including employee contribution rates, employer contribution rates, salary ceiling settings, and effective date management. Document social security enrollment processes, eligibility validation, and compliance requirements. Include configuration options for contribution rates, salary caps, and benefit calculations. Address common social security scenarios such as enrollment procedures, rate changes, and compliance reporting. Provide practical examples of social security setup workflows and integration with payroll calculation systems.

wiki_catalogs.name: Custom Hybrid Payroll
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the custom hybrid payroll mode that combines multiple calculation approaches. Document how this flexible mode allows mixing different payroll calculation methods within a single employee's compensation structure. Explain the override mechanisms, priority rules for different calculation sources, and how the system manages conflicts between various payroll modes. Include examples of common hybrid scenarios, configuration requirements, and best practices for implementing custom payroll combinations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Income Components Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for managing income components in monthly staff payroll. Document the five main income categories: base_salary, overtime_pay, diligence_allowance, performance_bonus, and other_income. Explain how each component is calculated, configured, and integrated into the total income calculation. Include examples of income item types, manual override capabilities, rule-based generation, and audit trail requirements for income adjustments.

wiki_catalogs.name: Attendance and Work Tracking
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the attendance and work tracking system. Describe the attendance log management functionality including check-in/check-out inputs, late minute tracking, early leave recording, and overtime calculation rules. Document the work log system for freelancers covering date-based entries, work type categorization, quantity/time measurements, layer rate calculations, and amount computations. Include configuration options for attendance rules, OT thresholds, grace periods, and deduction calculations. Explain integration with payroll calculation engine and how attendance data affects final pay amounts.

wiki_catalogs.name: Deduction Components Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for managing deduction components in monthly staff payroll. Document the five main deduction categories: cash_advance, late_deduction, lwop_deduction, social_security_employee, and other_deduction. Explain how each deduction is calculated, configured, and integrated into the total deduction calculation. Include examples of deduction item types, manual override capabilities, rule-based generation, and audit trail requirements for deduction adjustments. Address compliance requirements for each deduction type.

wiki_catalogs.name: Financial Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the financial management system covering both company finance tracking and payslip generation. Document the company finance module including revenue management, expense tracking, subscription cost management, and profit/loss reporting. Explain the payslip generation system including structure components, PDF creation workflow, snapshot mechanism, and finalization process. Include detailed coverage of financial reporting capabilities, annual summaries, tax simulation features, and compliance reporting requirements. Address integration between payroll calculations and financial summaries.

wiki_catalogs.name: Business Rules and Configuration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the business rules and configuration system. Thoroughly explain the rule-driven architecture including attendance rules (OT calculation, late deduction, LWOP), compensation rules (performance bonuses, threshold-based bonuses, layer rate calculations), and social security configuration (Thailand-specific rules). Document the rule management interface, configuration tables, and dynamic rule application. Include examples of rule setup, validation logic, and how rules cascade through the payroll calculation process. Address rule versioning, effective date management, and impact assessment procedures.

wiki_catalogs.name: User Interface and Experience
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the user interface and experience design. Describe the main interface components including Employee Board, Employee Workspace, Rule Manager, and Payslip Preview. Document the dynamic UI behaviors including inline editing capabilities, real-time calculation feedback, state management indicators, and responsive design patterns. Explain the grid-based payroll interface that mimics Excel functionality while maintaining proper data governance. Include user workflow documentation, accessibility considerations, and performance optimization for large datasets. Address mobile responsiveness and touch interaction patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Database Schema and Design
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive data model documentation for the database schema design. Detail the suggested table structure including users, roles, permissions, employees, payroll entities, attendance logs, work logs, and configuration tables. Document database conventions including naming standards, data types, indexing strategies, and foreign key relationships. Explain phpMyAdmin compatibility requirements, migration strategies, and schema evolution patterns. Include entity relationship diagrams, sample data structures, and performance optimization considerations. Address audit trail implementation, soft delete patterns, and data retention policies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Audit and Compliance System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the audit and compliance system. Document the audit trail implementation including mandatory logging fields (who, what, when, old/new values, action), high-priority audit areas, and compliance requirements. Explain the change management process, approval workflows, and rollback capabilities. Include detailed coverage of audit log structure, filtering and reporting capabilities, and integration with regulatory compliance requirements. Address data governance, retention policies, and forensic audit preparation. Document the relationship between audit logs and payslip finalization process.

wiki_catalogs.name: Development Guidelines
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive development guidelines covering coding standards, testing strategies, and best practices. Document PHP/Laravel development standards including service class organization, controller design patterns, validation approaches, and transaction management. Explain naming conventions, code organization principles, and architectural patterns. Include testing strategy documentation covering unit tests, integration tests, and business logic validation. Address code review processes, quality assurance procedures, and continuous integration practices. Document development workflow, branching strategies, and deployment procedures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Deployment and Maintenance
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for deployment and maintenance procedures. Document system requirements including PHP 8.2+, Laravel framework, MySQL 8+, and compatible hosting environments. Explain installation procedures, configuration management, and environment setup. Include migration and update procedures, database schema management, and data backup strategies. Address performance optimization guidelines, monitoring requirements, and troubleshooting common issues. Document maintenance procedures, security updates, and system health monitoring. Include deployment automation and rollback procedures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Change Management Process
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the change management process and development workflow. Document the five-question assessment framework for evaluating system changes including master vs monthly scope, affected payroll modes, impact on payslips/reports/finances, migration requirements, and audit/test coverage needs. Explain the approval workflow, risk assessment procedures, and rollback planning. Include guidelines for handling emergency changes, hotfix procedures, and regression testing requirements. Address version control practices, release management, and stakeholder communication procedures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Anti-Patterns and Best Practices
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content covering anti-patterns to avoid and best practices to follow in xHR system development. Document the 12 prohibited practices including Excel cell-based thinking, hardcoded values, business logic in views, and report-as-source-of-truth scenarios. Explain the rationale behind each anti-pattern and its potential consequences. Include detailed best practice guidelines for database design, service architecture, UI development, and audit implementation. Provide concrete examples of good vs bad implementations and decision-making frameworks for common development dilemmas.

wiki_catalogs.name: Minimum Deliverables and Definition of Done
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for minimum deliverables and definition of done criteria. Document the 12 minimum deliverables including project structure, database schema, migrations, seed data, model relationships, payroll services, rule manager, employee workspace UI, payslip builder/PDF, audit logs, annual summary, and company finance summary. Explain the definition of done checklist covering employee onboarding, payroll mode assignment, single-page salary entry, accurate calculations across all modes, SSO compliance, PDF generation, reporting capabilities, audit trails, and future maintainability. Include acceptance criteria and quality gates for each deliverable.

wiki_items.title: Minimum Deliverables and Definition of Done
Record-based Thinking Over Cell-based Excel Logic
Rule-driven Instead of Hardcoded Business Logic
Expense Claim and Company Financial Entities
Rate Rules and Layer Rate Rules
Module Toggle and System Configuration Rules

wiki_overview.content:
Based on my analysis of the project, this appears to be a comprehensive documentation file for a payroll and finance system project. Let me create a detailed analysis of this project.

<blog>

# xHR Payroll & Finance System - Comprehensive Project Analysis

## 1. Project Introduction

### Purpose Statement
The xHR Payroll & Finance System is a sophisticated payroll management solution designed to replace traditional Excel-based systems with a robust, database-driven application. This system automates and streamlines payroll processing for various employment types including monthly staff, freelancers, and content creators.

### Core Goals and Objectives
- **Modernization**: Replace outdated Excel spreadsheets with a structured database system
- **Automation**: Automate complex payroll calculations while maintaining flexibility
- **Compliance**: Ensure Thai social security compliance and regulatory adherence
- **Scalability**: Support multiple payroll modes and business rules
- **Auditability**: Maintain comprehensive audit trails for all financial transactions

### Target Audience
- HR departments managing multiple employee types
- Finance teams requiring automated payroll processing
- Organizations transitioning from manual spreadsheet-based systems
- Companies with diverse compensation structures including freelancers and content creators

## 2. Technical Architecture

### Component Breakdown

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[User Interface Layer] --> B[Controller Layer]
    B --> C[Service Layer]
    C --> D[Model Layer]
    D --> E[(MySQL Database)]
    
    F[Authentication Agent] --> G[Employee Management]
    H[Payroll Rules Agent] --> I[Payroll Engine]
    J[UI/UX Agent] --> K[Payslip Module]
    L[Audit Agent] --> M[Report Generation]
    
    N[PDF/Payslip Agent] --> O[Company Finance]
    P[Refactor Agent] --> Q[Rule Manager]
```

### Design Patterns
The system follows several key architectural patterns:

- **Service-Oriented Architecture**: Business logic encapsulated in dedicated service classes
- **Repository Pattern**: Data access abstraction through model relationships
- **Command Pattern**: Transactional operations wrapped in service actions
- **Observer Pattern**: Audit logging triggered by data changes

### System Relationships

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    A[Employee Board] --> B[Employee Workspace]
    B --> C[Payroll Engine]
    C --> D[Payslip Generator]
    D --> E[PDF Exporter]
    
    F[Rule Manager] --> C
    G[Audit System] --> H[Change Tracking]
    H --> I[Historical Records]
```
